title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Landscape connectivity — easily organisms, genes, ecological processes can move across landscape — central spatial ecology conservation biology. Circuit theory offers powerful way model . idea treat landscape electrical circuit: raster cell node, adjacent cells connected resistors, current flowing network reveals movement patterns. Unlike least-cost path methods, find single cheapest route, circuit theory considers possible pathways simultaneously. makes especially effective identifying movement corridors pinch points — places connectivity funneled narrow gaps. circuitscaper brings approach R wrapping two Julia tools: Circuitscape (computes connectivity specific sites) Omniscape (maps connectivity continuously across entire landscape). vignette provides basic overview circuitscaper R package. details circuit theory underlying Julia algorithms, see Circuitscape Omniscape documentation.

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: circuitscaper requires Julia (>= 1.9) Circuitscape Omniscape packages. can install everything R: already Julia installed, cs_setup() find automatically can point :
code: # Install circuitscaper (from GitHub during development) # remotes::install_github("matthewkling/circuitscaper")  library(circuitscaper)  # First time only: install Julia and required packages cs_install_julia() cs_setup(julia_home = "/path/to/julia/bin")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Every analysis starts resistance surface — raster cell’s value represents difficult organism process interest move location. typically derived land cover habitat suitability models. example, resistance surface forest-dwelling mammal might assign low values forest (easy traverse), moderate values grassland, high values urban areas water. (Note Circuitscape.jl Julia library allows resistance datasets specified adjacency graph networks, circuitscaper currently supports rasters.) Resistance values must positive. data represents conductance (ease movement) rather resistance, set resistance_is = "conductances".

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Circuitscape’s focal-node modes (pairwise, one--, --one) require focal nodes — sites want measure connectivity. typically habitat patches, populations, protected areas, sampling locations. can provide : matrix data.frame x/y coordinates (simplest) raster positive integer IDs site (0 NA ignored)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: circuitscaper provides four Circuitscape modes one Omniscape mode. ’s choose: Pairwise common starting point. Use discrete sites (habitat patches, populations) want know well-connected . resistance matrix useful distance metric analyses isolation resistance population genetics. One--iterates focal node, injecting current node grounding others. emphasizes current disperses outward source, making useful modeling recolonization potential identifying sites best overall connectivity network. --one reverse: nodes inject current node takes turn ground. emphasizes convergence useful identifying accessible reachable sites — example, protected areas easiest reach via migration. Advanced want full control source ground placement rather using focal nodes. provide explicit source current ground conductance rasters, single circuit solved. useful modeling directional movement defined source area destination (e.g., current flow migratory species’ winter summer ranges). Omniscape fundamentally different — doesn’t require focal nodes . uses moving window compute omnidirectional connectivity everywhere landscape. Use wall--wall connectivity mapping want identify corridors barriers across full extent.

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
code: library(terra) library(circuitscaper)  # Resistance surface (higher values = harder to traverse) resistance <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper"))  # Option 1: Focal nodes as coordinates (simplest) coords <- matrix(c(10, 40, 40, 40, 10, 10, 40, 10), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, coords)  # Option 2: Focal nodes as a raster (integer IDs; 0 and NA are not nodes) # locations <- rast("path/to/focal_nodes.tif") # result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations)  # Cumulative current map -- high values indicate important movement corridors plot(result$current_map)  # Pairwise resistance matrix -- can be used as a connectivity distance metric result$resistance_matrix

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
code: result <- cs_one_to_all(resistance, coords) plot(result) result <- cs_all_to_one(resistance, coords) plot(result$cumulative_current)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
code: source_layer <- rast(system.file("extdata/source.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) ground_layer <- rast(system.file("extdata/ground.tif", package = "circuitscaper"))  result <- cs_advanced(resistance, source_layer, ground_layer,                       ground_is = "conductances")  # Current density -- corridors and pinch points where flow is concentrated # i.e. possible preservation priorities plot(result[["current"]])  # Voltage -- analogous to movement probability, decreasing with distance # and resistance from sources plot(result[["voltage"]])  # Power dissipation -- areas of current flow through high-resistance areas, # i.e. possible restoration priorities plot(result[["current"]]^2 * resistance)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: cs_* functions expose several additional Circuitscape features.

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: default, pairwise, one--, --one modes return cumulative current map. Set cumulative_only = FALSE include per-pair (per-node) current layers, write_voltage = TRUE include voltage layers:
code: result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, coords,                       cumulative_only = FALSE,                       write_voltage = TRUE) names(result$current_map)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: default, focal node injects 1 amp current. Use source_strengths assign different injection strengths per node — useful sites differ population size habitat area:
code: # Strengths in the same order as the locations strengths <- c(2.5, 1.0, 0.5) result <- cs_one_to_all(resistance, coords, source_strengths = strengths)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Short-circuit regions represent areas zero resistance (e.g., lakes can crossed freely, developed areas funnel movement). Pass raster cells sharing positive integer value short-circuited:
code: polygons <- rast("path/to/short_circuit_regions.tif") result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations, short_circuit = polygons)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: large numbers focal nodes, may want restrict analysis subset pairs:
code: result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations,                       included_pairs = "path/to/pairs.txt")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Omniscape computes omnidirectional connectivity using moving window. require focal nodes — every cell can serve source. two key parameters : radius: moving window radius pixels. defines neighborhood connectivity evaluated — conceptually, far organism can disperse. Larger radii capture longer-distance connectivity increase computation time substantially. block_size: Aggregation factor source points (default 1, aggregation). Setting block_size = 3 groups sources 3x3 blocks, reducing number circuit solves typically negligible effects results. primary knob trading speed vs. precision. Omniscape returns three layers: cumulative_current: Raw current flow summed across moving-window iterations. Higher values indicate cells carry current overall. flow_potential: current flow look like resistance uniform everywhere. isolates effect source geometry landscape resistance. normalized_current: Cumulative current divided flow potential. Values greater 1 indicate corridors connectivity higher expected given arrangement sources; values less 1 indicate relative barriers. usually informative layer.
code: result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 20, block_size = 3) plot(result)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: default, source strength derived inverse resistance. can provide explicit source strength raster (e.g., habitat quality) weight sources independently resistance:
code: source_strength <- rast("path/to/habitat_quality.tif")  result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 20,                  source_strength = source_strength)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: large landscapes, enable Julia multithreading reduce compute times. Julia’s thread count fixed startup, set via cs_setup() circuitscaper calls:
code: # Set thread count at the start of your session cs_setup(threads = 4)  result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 20,                  block_size = 5,                  parallelize = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Circuitscape computation time scales number non-NA cells resistance raster , focal-node modes, number pairs iterations. practical guidelines: Raster size: Problems ~1 million cells run comfortably. larger landscapes, consider aggregating resistance surface analysis. Number focal nodes: Pairwise mode solves n*(n-1)/2 circuits, computation grows quadratically number nodes. Use included_pairs limit set needed. Omniscape: Computation dominated number source pixels radius. Use block_size reduce source count keep radius small ecologically justified. Enable parallelize = TRUE large runs. Solver choice: default "cg+amg" (iterative) solver works well large problems. small rasters (~10,000 cells), "cholmod" (direct solver) may faster.

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: default, intermediate files written temporary directory cleaned . persist output files (ASC rasters, INI configuration, resistance matrices), use output_dir:
code: result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations,                       output_dir = "my_output_directory")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: CRS preservation: circuitscaper captures CRS input raster reattaches outputs, even though Circuitscape’s ASCII grid format doesn’t carry CRS information. Conductance input: surface represents ease movement rather difficulty, set resistance_is = "conductances" avoid inverting values manually. ’s similar option grounds advanced mode. Verbose output: Set verbose = TRUE see Circuitscape’s solver progress, useful debugging monitoring long runs.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Matthew Kling. Author, maintainer.

what: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: circuitscaper provides streamlined R interface Circuitscape.jl Omniscape.jl. allows run high-performance landscape connectivity models entirely R, Julia handles heavy lifting hood. Native R Workflow: Input output terra raster objects directly. Automated Setup: One-line installation Julia required dependencies. High Performance: Leverages Julia’s state---art solvers large landscapes. Note: circuitscaper independent R package affiliated Circuitscape development team. lightweight wrapper excellent Julia tools developed Brad McRae, Viral Shah, Tanmay Mohapatra, Ranjan Anantharaman, collaborators.

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
code: # 1. Install the R package remotes::install_github("matthewkling/circuitscaper")  # 2. Let the package install Julia and the necessary Julia libraries library(circuitscaper) cs_install_julia()

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
code: library(circuitscaper) library(terra)  # Load an example resistance raster resistance <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper"))  # Pairwise Circuitscape # (result is a list containing the pairwise resistance matrix and current maps) focal_sites <- matrix(c(10, 40, 40, 40, 25, 10), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, focal_sites) plot(result$current_map) # Omniscape -- wall-to-wall moving-window connectivity # (result is a multi-layer SpatRaster of current flow variables) result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 10) plot(result$normalized_current)

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: R >= 4.0 Julia >= 1.9 (installed automatically via cs_install_julia()) R packages: terra, JuliaCall

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: Getting started vignette Circuitscape user guide Omniscape documentation McRae, B.H. (2006). Isolation resistance. Evolution, 60(8), 1551-1561. McRae, B.H. & Beier, P. (2007). Circuit theory predicts gene flow plant animal populations. PNAS, 104(50), 19885-19890. McRae, B.H., Dickson, B.G., Keitt, T.H. & Shah, V.B. (2008). Using circuit theory model connectivity ecology, evolution, conservation. Ecology, 89(10), 2712-2724.

what: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
title: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
text: Provides R-native interface Circuitscape.jl Omniscape.jl Julia packages landscape connectivity modeling using circuit theory. Users work entirely R familiar objects (SpatRaster, file paths) Julia handles computation invisibly. Supports four Circuitscape modes (pairwise, one--, --one, advanced) Omniscape moving-window analysis.

title: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
text: Maintainer: Matthew Kling mattkling@berkeley.edu

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Solve single circuit user-specified source ground layers.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
code: cs_advanced(   resistance,   source,   ground,   resistance_is = "resistances",   ground_is = "resistances",   use_unit_currents = FALSE,   use_direct_grounds = FALSE,   short_circuit = NULL,   source_ground_conflict = "keepall",   four_neighbors = FALSE,   avg_resistances = FALSE,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. source terra::SpatRaster file path. Source current strengths (amps per cell). Cells value 0 NA sources. ground terra::SpatRaster file path. Ground node values. Interpretation depends ground_is: resistances ground (default) conductances ground. Cells value 0 NA grounds. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". ground_is Character. Whether ground raster values represent "resistances" (default) "conductances" ground. use_unit_currents Logical. TRUE, current sources set 1 amp regardless values source raster. Default FALSE. use_direct_grounds Logical. TRUE, ground nodes tied directly ground (zero resistance), regardless values ground raster. Default FALSE. short_circuit Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Raster identifying short-circuit regions (aka polygons). Cells sharing positive integer value treated short-circuit regions zero resistance . Default NULL (short-circuit regions). source_ground_conflict Character. resolve cells appear source ground rasters: "keepall" (default, keep ), "rmvsrc" (remove source), "rmvgnd" (remove ground), "rmvall" (remove ). four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. avg_resistances Logical. using 8-neighbor connectivity, compute resistance diagonal connections average two cells rather sum. Default FALSE (Circuitscape default). Ignored four_neighbors = TRUE. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: current Current density cell. voltage Voltage cell. Voltage analogous movement probability decreases distance sources.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Unlike Circuitscape modes, advanced mode iterate focal nodes. Instead, user provides explicit source current ground conductance rasters, single circuit solved. gives full control current injection pattern useful modeling specific scenarios directional movement defined source area destination.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) origin <- rast(system.file("extdata/source.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) dest <- rast(system.file("extdata/ground.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) result <- cs_advanced(res, origin, dest, ground_is = "conductances") plot(result) } # }

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: focal node turn, inject current focal nodes ground single node.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
code: cs_all_to_one(   resistance,   locations,   resistance_is = "resistances",   four_neighbors = FALSE,   avg_resistances = FALSE,   short_circuit = NULL,   included_pairs = NULL,   write_voltage = FALSE,   cumulative_only = TRUE,   source_strengths = NULL,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. Use resistance_is argument surface represents conductances instead. locations Focal node locations, provided : terra::SpatRaster (raster::RasterLayer) positive integer IDs identifying node. Cells value 0 NA treated focal nodes. file path raster file (e.g., .tif, .asc). two-column matrix data.frame x/y coordinates. row becomes focal node, auto-assigned IDs 1, 2, 3, ... row order. Coordinates snapped nearest cell resistance raster. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. avg_resistances Logical. using 8-neighbor connectivity, compute resistance diagonal connections average two cells rather sum. Default FALSE (Circuitscape default). Ignored four_neighbors = TRUE. short_circuit Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Raster identifying short-circuit regions (aka polygons). Cells sharing positive integer value treated short-circuit regions zero resistance . Default NULL (short-circuit regions). included_pairs Optional character file path. text file specifying pairs focal nodes include exclude analysis. See Circuitscape documentation file format. Default NULL (pairs). write_voltage Logical. Write voltage maps. Default FALSE. TRUE, per-iteration voltage layers (named voltage_1, voltage_2, ...) included output raster. cumulative_only Logical. TRUE (default), cumulative current map returned. FALSE, per-iteration current layers (named current_1, current_2, ...) also included. Use caution large numbers focal nodes, can produce many layers. source_strengths Optional. Variable current injection strengths focal node. Can : numeric vector one value per focal node (order locations input). Node IDs assigned 1, 2, 3, ... matching order. character file path tab-delimited text file two columns: node ID strength amps. Nodes listed default 1 amp. Default NULL (nodes inject 1 amp). solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory cleaned automatically. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: cumulative_current Current flow summed across iterations. current_N Per-node current map focal node N, N integer node ID locations raster. One layer per focal node.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: --one mode iterates focal node. iteration, focal nodes injected 1 amp current , focal node connected ground. produces current map showing current converges node across landscape. mode useful identifying accessible reachable sites network, emphasizing current flow toward ground node. cumulative map sums across iterations highlights cells important connectivity across full set nodes.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) coords <- matrix(c(10, 40, 40, 40, 10, 10, 40, 10), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) result <- cs_all_to_one(res, coords) plot(result) } # }

what: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
title: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
text: Downloads installs Julia, Circuitscape.jl, Omniscape.jl. recommended first step installing circuitscaper R package.

title: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
code: cs_install_julia(force = FALSE, version = "latest")

title: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
text: force Logical. TRUE, reinstall Julia packages even appear already present. Default FALSE. version Character. Julia version install. Default "latest".

title: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success, FALSE cancelled.

title: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
text: interactive sessions, prompts confirmation downloading. non-interactive sessions (e.g., CI), proceeds without prompting.

title: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cs_install_julia() cs_install_julia(force = TRUE) } # }

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: focal node turn, inject current node ground focal nodes simultaneously.

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. Use resistance_is argument surface represents conductances instead. locations Focal node locations, provided : terra::SpatRaster (raster::RasterLayer) positive integer IDs identifying node. Cells value 0 NA treated focal nodes. file path raster file (e.g., .tif, .asc). two-column matrix data.frame x/y coordinates. row becomes focal node, auto-assigned IDs 1, 2, 3, ... row order. Coordinates snapped nearest cell resistance raster. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. avg_resistances Logical. using 8-neighbor connectivity, compute resistance diagonal connections average two cells rather sum. Default FALSE (Circuitscape default). Ignored four_neighbors = TRUE. short_circuit Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Raster identifying short-circuit regions (aka polygons). Cells sharing positive integer value treated short-circuit regions zero resistance . Default NULL (short-circuit regions). included_pairs Optional character file path. text file specifying pairs focal nodes include exclude analysis. See Circuitscape documentation file format. Default NULL (pairs). write_voltage Logical. Write voltage maps. Default FALSE. TRUE, per-iteration voltage layers (named voltage_1, voltage_2, ...) included output raster. cumulative_only Logical. TRUE (default), cumulative current map returned. FALSE, per-iteration current layers (named current_1, current_2, ...) also included. Use caution large numbers focal nodes, can produce many layers. source_strengths Optional. Variable current injection strengths focal node. Can : numeric vector one value per focal node (order locations input). Node IDs assigned 1, 2, 3, ... matching order. character file path tab-delimited text file two columns: node ID strength amps. Nodes listed default 1 amp. Default NULL (nodes inject 1 amp). solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory cleaned automatically. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: cumulative_current Current flow summed across iterations. current_N Per-node current map focal node N, N integer node ID locations raster. One layer per focal node.

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: One--mode iterates focal node. iteration, focal node injected 1 amp current remaining focal nodes simultaneously connected ground. produces current map showing current spreads node landscape reach others. mode useful mapping well site connected rest focal node network, emphasizing current dispersal source. cumulative map sums across iterations highlights cells important connectivity across full set nodes.

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) coords <- matrix(c(10, 40, 40, 40, 10, 10, 40, 10), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) result <- cs_one_to_all(res, coords) plot(result) } # }

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: named list : current_map terra::SpatRaster. default contains single cumulative_current layer (current flow summed across pairs). cumulative_only = FALSE, additional per-pair layers included (e.g., current_1_2, current_1_3). write_voltage = TRUE, per-pair voltage layers included (e.g., voltage_1_2, voltage_1_3). resistance_matrix symmetric numeric matrix pairwise effective resistances focal nodes, node IDs row column names.

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: Pairwise mode iterates every unique pair focal nodes. pair, one node injected 1 amp current connected ground. effective resistance pair recorded, resulting current flow accumulated across pairs cumulative current map highlights important movement corridors. common Circuitscape mode typically used quantify connectivity discrete habitat patches populations. resistance matrix can used distance metric analyses isolation resistance.

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) coords <- matrix(c(10, 40, 40, 40, 10, 10, 40, 10), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) result <- cs_pairwise(res, coords, cumulative_only = FALSE) plot(result$current_map) result$resistance_matrix } # }

what: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Initialize Julia session load Circuitscape Omniscape Julia packages. called automatically first use cs_* os_* function. Call explicitly control Julia path, number threads, pre-warm session.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
code: cs_setup(julia_home = NULL, threads = 1L, quiet = TRUE, ...)

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: julia_home Character. Path Julia bin/ directory. NULL (default), system PATH common locations searched. threads Integer. Number Julia threads start. Default 1L. Must set Julia initializes — Julia running, thread count changed without restarting R. setting controls parallelism os_run() ; Circuitscape functions (cs_*) run single-threaded regardless value. quiet Logical. Suppress Julia startup messages. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed JuliaCall::julia_setup().

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: cs_setup() install Julia Julia packages. Julia found required packages missing, throws informative error directing cs_install_julia(). cs_setup() : Verify Julia installed accessible. Verify Circuitscape Omniscape Julia packages installed. Load packages warm JIT compiler. Julia initialized, stays warm R session. Subsequent calls cs_setup() return immediately.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Julia's thread count fixed startup changed mid-session. Multi-threading used os_run() parallelize = TRUE. Circuitscape functions (cs_pairwise, cs_one_to_all, etc.) benefit multiple threads.
code: cs_setup(threads = 4) os_run(resistance, radius = 50, parallelize = TRUE)

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Circuitscape: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/ Omniscape: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Omniscape.jl/latest/

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cs_setup() cs_setup(threads = 4) cs_setup(julia_home = "/usr/local/julia/bin") } # }

what: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: Performs Omniscape analysis, computing omnidirectional landscape connectivity using moving window approach based circuit theory.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
code: os_run(   resistance,   radius,   source_strength = NULL,   block_size = 1L,   source_threshold = 0,   r_cutoff = Inf,   resistance_is = "resistances",   calc_normalized_current = TRUE,   calc_flow_potential = TRUE,   condition = NULL,   condition_type = NULL,   parallelize = FALSE,   julia_threads = 2L,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. radius Numeric. Moving window radius pixels. determines maximum distance connectivity evaluated source pixel. source_strength Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Source strength weights, often derived habitat quality suitability, higher values indicate stronger sources movement. NULL (default), source strength set inverse resistance (.e., non-nodata pixels become sources, weighted conductance). Use r_cutoff exclude high-resistance cells acting sources case. block_size Integer. Aggregation block size source points. Default 1 (aggregation). block_size e.g. 3 coarsens source grid 3x3 blocks, reducing number solves (thus computation time) substantially typically negligible effects results. source_threshold Numeric. Minimum source strength include pixel. Default 0. r_cutoff Numeric. Maximum resistance value cell included source source_strength = NULL. Cells resistance value excluded sources. Default Inf (cutoff). relevant source_strength provided. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". calc_normalized_current Logical. Compute normalized current flow. Default TRUE. calc_flow_potential Logical. Compute flow potential. Default TRUE. condition Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Conditional layer targeted connectivity analysis. condition_type Character. condition layer filters connectivity: "within" (connectivity source target cells whose condition values fall within specified range) "equal" (connectivity cells equal condition values, evaluated pairwise). relevant condition provided. Note: "within" currently uses Omniscape's default unbounded range (-Inf Inf), effectively includes cells. Finer control range bounds planned future version. parallelize Logical. Use Julia multithreading. Default FALSE. Julia's thread count fixed startup. Julia already initialized without enough threads, warning issued. avoid , call cs_setup() threads argument start session. julia_threads Integer. Number Julia threads parallelize = TRUE. Default 2. Ignored Julia already running fewer threads. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print Omniscape output. Default FALSE.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers (depending options): cumulative_current Raw cumulative current flow. Always present. Higher values indicate cells carry current across moving-window iterations. flow_potential Expected current homogeneous resistance (calc_flow_potential = TRUE). Reflects spatial configuration sources independently landscape resistance. normalized_current Cumulative current divided flow potential (calc_normalized_current = TRUE). Values greater 1 indicate cells connectivity higher expected given source geometry; values less 1 indicate relative barriers. typically informative layer identifying corridors pinch points.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: Omniscape documentation: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Omniscape.jl/latest/

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(system.file("extdata/resistance.tif", package = "circuitscaper")) result <- os_run(res, radius = 20) plot(result) } # }
